name: Fermented Foods
description: Lacto-fermentation producing more probiotic diversity than any supplement capsule — sauerkraut from cabbage + salt, water kefir from grains + sugar water, and kefir from grains + milk.
shelfLife: Sauerkraut: months refrigerated; kefir: 1–2 weeks; grains: indefinite with feeding
effectivenessNote: Superior probiotic diversity compared to supplements. Live cultures in naturally fermented foods contain hundreds of bacterial strains vs 5–15 in a capsule. One-time investment in grains provides infinite returns.
warnings: Use clean equipment to avoid contamination
Submerge vegetables below brine to prevent mold
Fermentation produces CO₂ — burp jars or use airlock lids
Sauerkraut needs 2% salt by weight — too little risks spoilage
scienceNote: Lactobacillus bacteria convert sugars to lactic acid, dropping pH to ~3.5 and preserving food while producing B vitamins, vitamin K2, and beneficial organic acids. The acidic environment selects for probiotic species while inhibiting pathogens.
